cards.text:
Introduction to Yoga
Yoga is a practice integrating physical, mental, and spiritual elements, originated in ancient India with the goal of harmonizing mind, body, and nature.

Meaning of Yoga
The word 'Yoga' is derived from the Sanskrit root 'yuj', meaning 'to join' or 'to unite', symbolizing the union of mind and body.

Sage Patanjali's Contribution
Sage Patanjali systematically introduced yoga in the 'Yoga Sutras of Patanjali', describing it as a tool to quieten the mind and eliminate distractions.

Yoga's Global Popularity
Worldwide, yoga is appreciated due to its numerous health benefits, contributing to its popularity across diverse cultures.

Benefits of Yoga: Physical Health
  • Yoga postures (asanas) stretch and strengthen muscles.
  • Improves flexibility, endurance, and balance.
  • Reduces body pains, like lower back pain.

Benefits of Yoga: Mental Health
  • Yoga includes breathing techniques and meditation.
  • Calms the mind, reduces stress, and alleviates anxiety.
  • Enhances sleep quality and concentration.

Yoga and the Immune System
Practicing yoga can enhance the immune system and promote heart health by lowering blood pressure and improving circulation.

Yoga for Weight Management
Engaging in yoga can aid weight loss by burning calories and maintaining overall health.

World Health Organisation (WHO) mYoga App
The WHO mYoga app, launched on 20 June 2021, offers videos and audios to facilitate yoga practice globally.

Mindful Practice of Yoga
Yoga requires coordinating each stretch with breath and awareness, highlighting the importance of practicing without distractions.
